payload.text: AI Interior Design Consultant: Build an interactive room makeover app. Users upload a photo of their current space, and the app offers a carousel of "reimagined" styles (e.g., Mid-Century Modern, Scandinavian) generated by AI. Implement a "Compare" slider that lets users drag between their original photo and the AI-generated design. Below the visualization, add a context-aware chat interface where the user can refine the design (e.g., "Keep this layout but make the rug blue") and get shoppable links for similar items.

**Conceptualizing the Interface**

I'm currently focused on the interface. The image upload and style carousel are clear, and I'm investigating how Gemini can best be integrated. Considering a visual, interactive experience for style selection to make the app more engaging for users. I'm keen on prioritizing easy navigation and fast image processing.


**Exploring Refinement Features**

I'm now diving into the user experience. A compare slider is being considered to easily view image variations. I'm excited about the context-aware chat, powered by `gemini-3-pro-preview`, to refine designs and provide shoppable links. Image editing via text prompts, using `gemini-2.5-flash-image`, will also be a key feature.


**Defining Core Structure**

I've been solidifying the architecture. I'm prioritizing a clean, well-defined state management system with key components: original and generated image handling, a chat history, and an `isLoading` flag. The services layer will house Gemini integrations for room design generation, editing, and consultation, using the specified models for efficiency and capability.


**Analyzing Shopping Integration**

I'm now investigating "shoppable links" and how to best integrate them. Initially, I thought standard text generation with the image would suffice for advice. However, upon closer review, it seems I should be using `gemini-3-pro-preview` with `googleSearch` for the chat to find actual items, as suggested.  I also realize that using the image context in the chatbot is important to search for shoppable links. For image generation, I'll use `gemini-3-pro-image-preview` with `googleSearch` to obtain real-time information.


**Incorporating Search & Structure**

I've been working on integrating Google Search into the text chat to deliver "shoppable links," leveraging `gemini-3-pro-preview` with the `googleSearch` tool. Regarding components, I've outlined the core UI elements, including an image uploader, style carousel, comparison slider, chat interface, and loading overlay. Additionally, I am currently exploring initial image generation options and prompt parsing for edits.

**Pinpointing Gemini Models**

I'm now determining the best Gemini models for each function.  For image generation and editing, `gemini-2.5-flash-image` seems ideal, handling image input alongside text prompts. The chat feature will use `gemini-3-pro-preview`, incorporating image history if feasible, and the crucial `googleSearch` tool for delivering shoppable links.



**Confirming Implementation Details**

I'm solidifying the implementation. Confirmed constraints include avoiding `react-dropzone`, using `gemini-2.5-flash-image` for editing, `gemini-3-pro-preview` for chat, and Tailwind. I'm focusing on a clean, light-mode design with a layout split between a visualizer (left/top) and chat/controls (right/bottom). The comparison slider will be a `div` with background images. For chat, I'm considering sending image context to the `gemini-3-pro-preview` model for enhanced shoppable link generation.


**Parsing Grounding Data**

I'm now integrating the Grounding data from `gemini-3-pro-preview` with `googleSearch`. Displaying the sources, from `groundingMetadata`, is becoming a priority. I'm streamlining the chat interface to handle Markdown (Gemini output) and links. I'm leaning towards simple regex for links to avoid a heavy markdown library, focusing on presenting information clearly. The `geminiService` will be refined for this chat functionality, specifically deciding whether user inputs are chat messages or commands.


**Integrating Edit Modes**

I'm now focusing on resolving user intent for image editing. I've developed a heuristic: explicitly toggling an "Edit Mode" or using the LLM to classify intent. Simplifying for the React app, I'm considering a "Generate/Update" button or letting the Chat LLM make the call. The most "AI" approach is via Function Calling. My tool will be `edit_image(prompt: string)`. If a user says "Make it blue", the Chat LLM will call `edit_image("Make the room have a blue rug")`. Frontend intercepts this, calls `gemini-2.5-flash-image`, receives, and updates state.


**Implementing Function Calling**

I've decided on function calling for the chat to drive image generation. The tool will be `updateDesign({ description: string })`. The React app's state will update to "Generating..." when triggered, then call the image API, and display the result. This simplifies the user experience. I'll use a prompt like "Interior design visualization of a room. [User Prompt]. Photorealistic, high quality." for image generation. I will use `lucide-react` for the UI icons.


**Building Base Structure**

I'm now focusing on setting up the basic React application structure, creating the file structure and laying the groundwork for the initial components. I've merged some small components to keep things organized. The `geminiService` will handle both Chat and Image. I'm prioritizing the Image Upload component next. I'll need to figure out how to manage API keys effectively and how to handle the different API request structures for Gemini's image and chat models.


**Refining the Chat Functionality**

I'm presently focusing on the chat's functionality. I'm prioritizing direct integration of the function calls to update the image. Handling the "function call" responses from the chat model and triggering the `generateImage` service, with the original or currently-active image as input, is what I'm working on right now.


I've just been thinking about how to refine images without losing quality. Using the original image as a reference is crucial, and now I'm experimenting with maintaining a `currentDesignPrompt` state.  My thinking is, the prompt "A photo of a room" is used initially, then gets updated. I am also thinking about how the model works and the quality degradation when you use generated images as inputs.


**Analyzing Prompt Handling**

I'm now zeroing in on prompt handling. I'm focusing on "prompt chaining," where I refine the original prompt progressively, storing each version. This means the model works effectively with evolving instructions. The challenge now is deciding how to store the prompts; perhaps a list? I'm weighing whether to include image context with each prompt or just the latest version.
